hypothesis: Incorporating three-dimensional conformational information—such as atomic coordinates derived from low-energy conformer ensembles encoded as a structured textual representation (e.g., serialized distance matrices or torsion angle sequences)—as an eighth modality within the STR-Bamba unified vocabulary will produce a latent space cluster that is geometrically distinct from all seven existing textual representations yet exhibits measurable cross-modal alignment with SMILES and InChI embeddings, thereby improving prediction accuracy for conformation-dependent properties (e.g., protein-ligand binding affinity, membrane permeability) beyond what any combination of the current seven representations achieves.
